domain: Reinforcement Learning for SLM Reasoning (GRPO/RLVR Tools)

gaps.title: Reward Function Marketplace / Reusable Reward Library

demand_signals.signal: TRL issue #2644 'GRPO reward func' — users confused about reward function implementation patterns
TRL issue #2771 'Question about reward function in GRPO example' — docs unclear on reward design
Users note 'designing verifiable reward functions can be tough, and so most examples are math or code' — domain-specific rewards are hard to design
GRPO++ article dedicates section to reward function design as the primary bottleneck for practitioners
arxiv:2602.09305 — 'Reward Modeling for RL-Based LLM Reasoning: Design, Challenges, and Evaluation' — entire paper dedicated to reward design challenges

gaps.title: Curriculum / Difficulty Scheduling for RL Training

demand_signals.signal: TRL issue #3668 'GRPOTrainer reward functions cannot access training progress' — users want dynamic rewards that change with training stage
E2H Reasoner (arxiv:2506.06632) shows curriculum RL requires fewer total samples — clear efficiency gap
VCRL (arxiv:2509.19803) proposes variance-based curriculum — reward variance as difficulty proxy, not implemented in tools
Practitioner notes 'GRPO starts off slow, with super small loss because rewards on all observations are the same' — difficulty scheduling would help

gaps.title: One-Click GRPO Training for Non-ML Engineers

demand_signals.signal: Users ask 'is GRPO practical?' and question whether it's easier to just use frontier models — accessibility is a concern
Unsloth Studio launched March 2026 targeting no-code fine-tuning — validates demand for accessible RL training
Multiple 'how to train your reasoning model' tutorials indicate high interest from non-experts wanting step-by-step guidance
TRL issue #2731 'Latest TRL code = significantly worse rewards for GRPO training' — users can't diagnose what changed
Entropy collapse identified as 'silent killer of RLVR generalization' — no tool detects this automatically
GRPO homogeneous response problem causes null gradients — no tool flags this during training
GRPO++ article lists multiple training stability tricks that must be manually monitored
K2V framework (Knowledge-to-Verification) extends RLVR to knowledge-intensive domains — research validates the need
RLVR shown effective for medical QA (MedQA-USMLE) and science — demand exists but tooling doesn't
Label Studio created RLVR templates for annotation but not for training — market adjacent demand
Open-RS paper: 'RL for Reasoning in Small LLMs: What Works and What Doesn't' — dedicated AAAI paper on this gap
DeepScaleR 1.5B surpassing O1-Preview demonstrates small-model potential — but tooling is research-grade
Hardware resource dependency noted — practitioners want RL training on consumer GPUs
Unsloth focuses on VRAM efficiency for small models — 5GB VRAM for 1.5B — proving market demand

gaps.title: RL Training Cost Estimator and Resource Planner

demand_signals.signal: Predictive Scaling Laws for GRPO (July 2025) exist as research but not implemented in any tool
RL training spends 80% of compute on generation — generation parameters dominate cost unpredictably
Cost-benefit analysis is top concern — users question if GRPO is worth the compute vs. distillation
ROLL from Alibaba supports multi-task RL but requires Alibaba ecosystem — demand exists but locked in
Guru dataset covers 6 reasoning domains — validates need for cross-domain training

gaps.title: RL Training Data Curation and Quality Pipeline

demand_signals.signal: Practitioners must 'think about problem setting, generate data, choose base model, design reward functions' — data curation is manual
SimpleRL shows 8K examples suffice for 10-20 point gains — but finding the right 8K requires expertise
Open-R1 focuses entirely on data transparency and curation for reasoning models

gaps.title: Automated Reward Hacking Detection and Mitigation

demand_signals.signal: Anthropic paper: 'Natural emergent misalignment from reward hacking in production RL' — major safety concern
arxiv:2602.18037 — 'Gradient Regularization Prevents Reward Hacking in RLHF and Verifiable Rewards' — active research area
Comprehensive reward hacking analysis notes 'not a ton of work on mitigations, especially for RLHF and LLMs'

gaps.title: RL-Specific Model Evaluation Suite for Reasoning

demand_signals.signal: Promptfoo: 'RLVR Makes Models Faster, Not Smarter' — evaluation tools can't distinguish efficiency vs capability
'Does RL Really Incentivize Reasoning Beyond Base Model?' — current benchmarks can't answer this
arxiv:2510.10541 'Rethinking RL Evaluation: Can Benchmarks Truly Reveal Failures?' — systematic evaluation gap
Blog comparing RL libraries notes each has different APIs, making switching costly
TRL issue #3157 'GRPO with vLLM is blocked' — integration/compatibility issues frequent

gaps.title: Process Reward Model (PRM) Training and Serving Toolkit

demand_signals.signal: RM-R1: Reward Modeling as Reasoning — proposes chain-of-rubrics but no accessible implementation
Process-supervised reward models shown to reduce reward hacking — but training them is prohibitively expensive

gaps.title: RL Training for Agentic Reasoning (Tool Use + Reasoning)

demand_signals.signal: OpenRLHF 0.8.0 added async agent RLHF — feature demand drove implementation
ROLL ROME from Alibaba introduced IPA algorithm for agentic model training
SkyRL from Berkeley targets multi-turn agentic training — new project indicating demand
Cost-benefit analysis is primary concern — users ask if GRPO worth the compute
No fine-tuning API from Together/Fireworks/Anyscale supports GRPO — market gap
